name: Old-Fashioned Buttermilk Pound Cake
recipeIngredient: Martha White All-Purpose Flour, sifted (3 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
2. Grease and flour a 10-inch tube pan.
3. Cream together butter, shortening, and sugar in a mixing bowl until light and fluffy.
4. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
5. Blend in vanilla extract.
6. Sift together flour, salt, baking powder, and baking soda.
7. Alternately add the sifted dry ingredients with buttermilk to the creamed mixture, beginning and ending with the dry mixture. Mix well after each addition.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
9. Bake for 1 hour and 15 minutes or until a toothpick inserted one inch from the edge comes out clean.
10. Allow cooling in the pan for 10 minutes before turning out onto a wire rack to cool completely.
